Title: The rice mitochondrial iron transporter is essential for plant growth

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for mitochondrial electron transport, heme synthesis, and Fe-S cluster synthesis; essential for plant growth and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes mitochondrial Fe import; loss or reduction causes decreased mitochondrial Fe, impaired Fe-S cluster-related aconitase activity, abnormal shoot Fe overaccumulation, severe growth defects, reduced fertility and yield, and knockout lethality
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MIT (Os03g0296800) was identified from a rice T-DNA screen as the causal gene for an Fe-deficiency-like phenotype with elevated shoot Fe. 2. MIT encodes a mitochondrial solute carrier family protein localized to mitochondria and functionally complements the yeast Δmrs3Δmrs4 mitochondrial Fe transport mutant. 3. Homozygous MIT knockout was lethal, while MIT knockdown reduced mitochondrial Fe by ~49% and shoot aconitase activity/mitochondrial aconitase activity by ~39%/~42%, indicating impaired Fe-S cluster synthesis. 4. MIT knockdown plants accumulated more shoot Fe yet showed reduced growth, chlorophyll, tillers, fertility, and yield; complementation with MITp-MIT rescued these defects.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mitochondrial solute carrier (MSC) family protein with conserved carrier regions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and shoots; induced by excess Fe and reduced under Fe limitation; expressed during germination (embryo, leaf primordia, coleorhizae), seed development, and throughout multiple organs including root, leaves, stem, anther, pistil, lemma, palea, ovary, embryo and endosperm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In rice roots and shoots under Fe sufficiency/excess; embryo during germination; developing seeds; broad expression across vegetative and reproductive tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fe deficiency (0 μM Fe), Fe sufficiency (100 μM Fe), excess Fe (500 μM Fe); hydroponic and soil growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout and knockdown mutant analysis; complementation of yeast Δmrs3Δmrs4 mutant; GFP subcellular localization in tobacco BY-2 cells; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS expression; metal profiling of shoots and isolated mitochondria; aconitase activity assay; genetic complementation with MITp-MIT in rice
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray (Agilent rice 44K oligo-DNA microarray), GEO: GSE28428; rice global gene expression profile datasets
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MIT promoter (1.7 kb; endogenous promoter) for GUS and complementation constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced MIT expression causes severe growth penalty, chlorosis, delayed flowering, fewer tillers, reduced fertility, ~59% yield reduction, and callus growth defects; complete loss is lethal
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MIT is a transporter, not an enzyme or transcription factor, so classified as Common Gene. MIT expression in yeast reverted FIT2 and FET3 upregulation and reversed Fe/Cu accumulation changes in Δmrs3Δmrs4. Mn accumulation was also altered in mit-2. The study positions MIT as a major mitochondrial Fe transporter essential for rice Fe homeostasis and development.
